name: extend-the-topic-editor-s-20260514-0315
idea:
Extend the topic editor's read tools so the agent can investigate context beyond the current source window. Today the read tools (`search_messages`, `get_author_profile`, `get_message_context`) are scoped to the in-memory source window built at run start. The agent can't ask "what was this a reply to?" or "what has this author been working on for the past week?" unless the relevant messages happen to fall in the current hour.

# Three new capabilities

Add THREE new read tools to `TOPIC_EDITOR_TOOLS` in `src/features/summarising/topic_editor.py`. Keep the four existing read tools as-is.

## 1. `search_archive_messages`

A full-archive variant of `search_messages` — queries `discord_messages` directly, not the source window.

```python
{
    "name": "search_archive_messages",
    "description": "Search archived Discord messages across the full archive (not limited to the current source window). Use when you need broader context — e.g. 'all messages mentioning Wan 2.5 from this author in the past 7 days', or 'what was discussed in #animatediff yesterday'.",
    "input_schema": {
        "type": "object",
        "properties": {
            "query": {"type": "string"},
            "channel_id": {"type": "integer"},
            "author_id": {"type": "integer"},
            "hours_back": {"type": "integer", "description": "default 168 (1 week), max 720 (30 days)"},
            "limit": {"type": "integer", "description": "default 20, max 50"},
        },
        "required": ["query"],
    },
}
```

Implementation: new storage method `search_archive_messages(query, *, guild_id, environment, channel_id, author_id, hours_back, limit)` that ILIKEs `content` in `discord_messages`, filters by guild + optional channel/author, time-windowed via `created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '{hours_back} hours'`. Cap limit at 50, hours_back at 720. Return `[{message_id, channel_id, channel_name, author_id, author_name, content_preview, created_at, reply_to_message_id}]`.

## 2. `get_reply_chain`

Walks `reply_to_message_id` from a given message back to the chain root (or until `max_depth` is hit). Useful for "what was this generation a reply to?"

```python
{
    "name": "get_reply_chain",
    "description": "Walk the reply chain backwards from a message. Returns the ancestor messages in order from root to direct-parent. Stops at chain root or max_depth.",
    "input_schema": {
        "type": "object",
        "properties": {
            "message_id": {"type": "string"},
            "max_depth": {"type": "integer", "description": "default 5, max 15"},
        },
        "required": ["message_id"],
    },
}
```

Implementation: iterative loop following `reply_to_message_id` in `discord_messages`, with cycle detection (track seen IDs in a set, abort if revisit). Cap at 15. Each step is one query — fine at depth 5. Return `[{message_id, author_id, author_name, channel_id, content_preview, created_at}]` ordered root-first.

## 3. `get_author_recent_messages`

Author timeline from the full archive — recent activity to ground "they've been iterating on X."

```python
{
    "name": "get_author_recent_messages",
    "description": "Fetch an author's recent messages across the full archive (not limited to the current source window). Use to understand what they've been working on, what threads they're in, what tools/techniques they've been testing.",
    "input_schema": {
        "type": "object",
        "properties": {
            "author_id": {"type": "integer"},
            "hours_back": {"type": "integer", "description": "default 168 (1 week), max 720"},
            "limit": {"type": "integer", "description": "default 20, max 50"},
            "channel_id": {"type": "integer", "description": "optional, filter to a specific channel"},
        },
        "required": ["author_id"],
    },
}
```

Implementation: storage method `get_author_recent_messages(author_id, *, guild_id, environment, hours_back, limit, channel_id)`. Query `discord_messages` ordered by `created_at DESC`, time-windowed, optional channel filter. Return same shape as `search_archive_messages` results.

# Plumbing

For all three:
- Add to `TOPIC_EDITOR_TOOLS` (the existing list)
- Add dispatcher cases in `_dispatch_read_tool` — same shape as the existing read tools, JSON-encoded result capped at ~2KB so tool_result doesn't blow up token budget
- Add storage methods (async) in `src/common/storage_handler.py`
- Add db_handler wrappers (sync via `_run_async_in_thread`) in `src/common/db_handler.py`
- Tests in `tests/test_topic_editor_runtime.py` exercising each new tool with the fake store

# Update the system prompt

Add a paragraph teaching the agent these three tools exist and when to use them:
- `search_archive_messages` when "the in-window search doesn't have what I need"
- `get_reply_chain` whenever a source message has `reply_to_message_id` set and the context matters
- `get_author_recent_messages` whenever sharing a generation — to ground the "what they've been working on" framing

# Hard scope guards

- Do NOT modify the four existing read tools (`search_topics`, `search_messages`, `get_author_profile`, `get_message_context`) — those stay scoped to the source window
- Do NOT touch `src/features/summarising/live_update_editor.py` or `live_update_prompts.py`
- Don't change `finalize_run`, the agent loop, override-collision, idempotency, lease-expiry, force-close, vision tools, or any prior subagent's work
- Existing tests must remain green

overrides.reason: switching to unified Discord-style search shape
